sections.paragraph:
i.q. סוּךְ (compare No. 3) to pour, to pour out, Isa 29:10, specially-

(1) in honour of a god, to make a libation, απένδειν, Exo 30:9 Hos 9:4. Whence Isa 30:1, נְסֹךְ מַסֵּכָה σπένδεσθαι σπονδήν, to make a covenant, because the ancients in making covenants were accustomed to offer libations, (compare Lat. spondere, from σπονδή).

(2) to cast out of metal, Isa 40:19 44:10 .

(3) to anoint a king, Psa 2:6. Compare נְסִיךְ No. 3.

Niphal pass. of No. 3, to be anointed, Pro 8:23.

Piel i.q. Kal No. 1, to make a libation, 1Ch 11:18 Syr. Pa. id. In the parallel place, 2Sa 23:16 there is Hiph. which is more used in the older Hebrew.

Hiphil id. to pour out (libations), to make a libation, Gen 35:14 Num 28:7 Jer 7:18 Psa 16:4.

Hophal pass. Exo 25:29 37:16 .

Derived nouns, מַסֵּכָה No. I. נָסִיךְ, נֶסֶךְ.

II.

נסך

i.q. cogn. סָכַךְ.-

(1) to intertwine, to weave, to hedge, i.q. Arab. نسح, hence מַסֶּכָת the warp. From the idea of hedging, fencing, comes-

(2) to cover, to protect, Isa 25:7. Comp. מַסֵּכָה No. II.
